user_query:
I'm a retired teacher who helps run cultural activities at our guesthouse. Please set up a small, self-contained daily "What's On Today" bulletin generator that I can schedule to run automatically each morning.

Inputs (already provided in this workspace):
- config/config.yaml: contains the morning run time, output directory, and which event categories to include in the bulletin.
- input/events.csv: the master list of events.
- tools/validate_events.py: a simple validator that emits warnings to stderr for any event rows with formatting problems (e.g., a date not in YYYY-MM-DD). You must use this to detect rows to exclude.

Requirements:
1) Create a script at scripts/generate_bulletin.py that:
   - Reads config/config.yaml (do not hardcode run time, categories, or output directory; use the config values).
   - Calls tools/validate_events.py on input/events.csv, captures its stderr, and parses any warnings for "event_id=...". Any event_ids reported by the validator must be excluded from the bulletin and noted in the run log.
   - Extracts structured data from input/events.csv and filters events by an explicit date argument --date YYYY-MM-DD (if omitted, use the system date) and by the category list in the config (case-insensitive match). Sort included events by start_time.
   - Writes a human-readable bulletin text file to {output_dir}/bulletin-YYYY-MM-DD.txt where {output_dir} comes from the config. Each line should include start_time, title, category, and location (e.g., "09:00 - Morning Ragas (Music) @ Main Hall").
   - Also write a machine-readable summary CSV to out/summaries/summary-YYYY-MM-DD.csv with columns: event_id,title,category,start_time.
   - Produce a run log at out/logs/run-YYYY-MM-DD.log that begins with a line "Skipped event_ids: [comma-separated list]" (empty list if none), followed by a short one-paragraph explanation of what the validator warnings mean, and then a copy of the raw stderr from the validator.

2) Scheduling deliverable (do not actually install cron):
   - Read run_time (HH:MM) from config/config.yaml and create a single-line cron entry that runs the generator once daily at that time. The command should invoke the script using an absolute path based on the current workspace and redirect both stdout and stderr to out/logs/cron.log. Save that one-line entry to out/cron_entry.txt. The scheduled command must not pass --date so the script uses the system date when run by cron.

3) Demonstration run (deterministic for verification):
   - Run your script once with --date 2024-07-15 and produce these artifacts:
     - config.output_dir/bulletin-2024-07-15.txt
     - out/summaries/summary-2024-07-15.csv
     - out/logs/run-2024-07-15.log
   Ensure that any event rows flagged by tools/validate_events.py are excluded and their event_id values appear in the "Skipped event_ids" line.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not modify the provided input files.
- Use only standard libraries; do not require installing external packages.
- Keep everything local; do not install cron—just write the cron line to out/cron_entry.txt.
- Make path handling robust so the cron line will work when pasted into a user crontab.

What I expect to find afterward:
- The script at scripts/generate_bulletin.py.
- The generated out/cron_entry.txt with one cron line using the time from the config.
- For the test date 2024-07-15: the bulletin text, the summary CSV, and a run log that clearly shows which event_ids were skipped and why. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
